meta.title: Leviathan Mission Candidates — Verification Report
meta.description: Ground-truth code verification of 5 mission candidates for the Leviathan agent runtime

props.title: Mission Candidate Verification
props.subtitle: Ground-truth code analysis across 5 candidates for the next Leviathan mission

props.content: Workers inspected actual source files, dependency graphs, and TODO markers to separate what's real from what's assumed. Every claim below is backed by file paths and line numbers.

props.label: CANDIDATE 1
props.title: Runtime Truth Wave 2 — Receipt Emission
props.subtitle: Wire immutable completion proofs into the iterative runner and heartbeat FSM

props.content: Wave 1 delivered production-quality receipt infrastructure — types, factory, 9 test cases, policy enforcement, witness accumulator. Wave 2 has ZERO code written. The injection points are waiting.

props.title: Receipt Types
props.content: `core/orchestration/src/receipt/types.ts` — Receipt, ReceiptScope ('iteration', 'tick', 'session'), ReceiptProvenance, CreateReceiptOptions

props.title: Receipt Factory
props.content: `core/orchestration/src/receipt/factory.ts` — createReceipt() with `rcpt-` prefixed IDs, immutable output

props.title: 9 Test Cases
props.content: `receipt.test.ts` — factory correctness, immutability, accumulation, embedding, real-world scenario

props.title: Policy Enforcement
props.content: `receipt-policy.ts` — enforceReceiptPolicy(), DEFAULT and STRICT policy presets

props.title: Witness Accumulator
props.content: `witness-accumulator.ts` — getByReceipt() for receipt-linked witness sets

props.title: DNA Updates
props.content: `dna/graph.yaml` execution_primitives + `dna/vernacular.yaml` — receipt as 7th primitive

rows.evidence: Writes raw JSONL, no receipt wrapping
ADR says adapter needed, none exists

props.title: Natural injection points exist
props.content: ReceiptScope includes 'iteration' and 'tick'. The iterative runner has onIterationEvent and emitLifecycleEvent hooks. Heartbeat has onTick and onSession callbacks. createReceipt() is already in the public API. This is additive wiring with no breaking changes.

props.label: CANDIDATE 2
props.title: Event Bus Wiring Sprint
props.subtitle: Connect pending event emissions across core/workstream, core/harness, and core/domain

props.content: The event bus is fully functional with pub/sub, JSONL persistence, bridge layer, and 10+ working emitters. This is pure connect-the-dots wiring — the patterns already exist in core/exec and core/daemon.

props.title: Event bus is mature and production-ready
props.content: EventBus (LifecycleEvent-based) + LevEvent (JSONL persistence) + InProcessEventBus (test/standalone). Glob-style pattern matching, in-memory history (10K events), optional disk persistence. Bridges: LifecycleEvent to LevEvent, Footer to LifecycleEvent, SCM to LevEvent, JSONL tail to dispatch.

props.title: 3 ready, 2 blocked, 1 out of scope
props.content: The 3 clean tasks follow established patterns from core/exec (emitLevEvent) and core/daemon. BD hook needs a BD client dependency (separate concern). Token attribution gated by W2 caller migration. AdapterRegistry is infrastructure, not event emission.

props.label: CANDIDATE 3
props.title: FlowMind for_each — Collection Iteration
props.subtitle: The #1 missing capability per NAAC dogfooding, but with significant design ambiguity

props.content: FlowMind doesn't support for item in collection yet. I wrote a bash script to do the outer loop. That works, but it means the orchestration is split: FlowMind handles the inner graph, bash handles iteration. This is exactly the kind of thing FlowMind should handle natively. — NAAC Dogfooding Report

props.title: Exists Today
props.content: **Until-loop** (repeat until condition, circuit breaker, idle detection). GraphNode has unused `foreach?` field — parser recognizes it but executor ignores it. LoopExecutor injection pattern via AdapterEnv. Execution contract handles `kind: 'loop'`. Parallel execution with concurrency limits works.

props.title: Needs Creation
props.content: **Collection iteration** — zero support anywhere. Domain type additions (GraphNode fields, InstructionKind). Collection resolver (shell to array). Execution contract for_each detection. ForEachExecutor interface + fulfillForEach(). Collection runner with concurrency. ~1,710 LOC across 8-10 features.

rows.question: Collection source
rows.options: Shell output? Knob array? File? JSONL? All?

rows.question: Relationship to parallel
rows.options: Dynamic targets from collection? New topology?

props.title: Viable but needs a design doc first
props.content: The infrastructure is solid and the pain point is real. But building without resolving these 7 questions risks building the wrong thing. A 1-page design doc answering each question would unblock implementation. The NAAC report even includes proposed YAML syntax.

props.label: CANDIDATE 4
props.title: Architecture Violation Cleanup (V5 + V7)
props.subtitle: Documented delete-safe cleanup of execSync anti-patterns

props.content: Delete 3 lines (barrel, test, config) and the system is whole. — ARCHITECTURE.md section 13, on V5

safe.text: YES — no CLI route, no consumers
NO — too complex for this scope

props.title: Full inventory: 73 execSync/spawnSync sites across 22 files
props.content: The codebase has significant execSync debt beyond V5+V7. But those two are cleanly scoped: V5 is an orphaned handler with no surface route (genuinely dead code), and V7 is a 15-line function that was extracted FROM harness TO orchestration and needs to go back. Neither has hidden dependencies.

props.title: Caveat: no_poly_handlers gate won't pass
props.content: Deleting cdo.ts removes 1 of 24 handler files in core/poly/src/handlers/. The no_poly_handlers validation gate will still fail. But it's tracked separately with an active alignment session (poly-design-spec-alignment).

props.label: CANDIDATE 5
props.title: Plugin config.yaml Remediation
props.subtitle: Not a mission — the gate violation count is stale and most targets are dead code

props.title: Actually 6 violations, not 3 — gate count is stale
props.content: The validation gate says violations_known: 3 but there are 6 directories missing config.yaml. 2 are dead code (delete), 3 are empty placeholders (decide intent), 1 needs a 10-line file. Total effort: 15 minutes. Mission overhead would exceed the work.
Three candidates are mission-ready. One needs design first. One isn't a mission at all.

items.title: #1 Runtime Truth Wave 2
items.description: Priority 0 plan, solid Wave 1 foundation, clear scope (5-7 features), natural injection points. Strategic + achievable.

items.title: #2 Event Bus Wiring
items.description: Only 3 clean features (~50 LOC total). Could be bundled into a larger mission or done as a quick standalone sprint.

items.title: #4 V5+V7 Cleanup
items.description: 3 features, genuinely small. Delete dead code + move a function. Could bundle with #2 for a solid small mission.

items.title: #3 FlowMind for_each
items.description: Highest impact long-term, but 7 unresolved design questions. Run a design session first.

items.title: #5 Plugin config.yaml
items.description: 15-minute cleanup. Delete dead dirs, add one config.yaml. Not worth mission infrastructure.
